name: Tweet Reminders Cloud Migration
description: Convert local-only Chrome extension to a cloud-backed product with auth, synced reminders, web push notifications, a dashboard, and a marketing page. Monorepo with pnpm, dockerized for Hetzner deployment.

tasks.title: Convert to pnpm monorepo
tasks.description: Initialize pnpm workspace at repo root. Move existing extension code into apps/extension with its own package.json. Create apps/web for the Next.js app. Set up shared packages/types workspace for Reminder types shared between extension and web. Update all build scripts and ensure the extension still builds correctly from its new location.

tasks.title: Scaffold Next.js app
tasks.description: Create apps/web as a Next.js 15 app with App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4. Configure path aliases. This will host the API routes, marketing page, dashboard, and auth flows.

tasks.title: Shared types package
tasks.description: Create packages/shared with the Reminder interface, API request/response types, and Web Push subscription types. Both apps/extension and apps/web import from this package. Migrate existing src/shared/types.ts here and extend with userId, server-side fields (e.g., Reminder gains a userId, drops notified which becomes client-side only).

tasks.title: Database schema and setup
tasks.description: Set up a Postgres connection in apps/web using the pg library with a simple query helper. Define tables via hand-written SQL migrations in a migrations/ directory: users (id, email, created_at), reminders (id, user_id, tweet_url, tweet_id, tweet_text, author_handle, reminder_time, created_at), push_subscriptions (id, user_id, endpoint, p256dh, auth, created_at). Write a minimal migration runner script that applies .sql files in order. Write the initial 001_init.sql migration.

tasks.title: Authentication with better-auth + Resend magic links
tasks.description: Install and configure better-auth in apps/web. Set up magic link provider using Resend for email delivery. Create auth API route handler at /api/auth/[...all]. Create login page at /login with email input for magic link. Configure session management. The extension will authenticate by opening the login page in a new tab and receiving the session token back.

tasks.title: Reminders CRUD API routes
tasks.description: Create Next.js API routes: GET /api/reminders (list user's reminders), POST /api/reminders (create), DELETE /api/reminders/[id] (delete), PATCH /api/reminders/[id] (update, e.g. snooze). All routes require authenticated session. Validate inputs. Return proper error responses.

tasks.title: Web Push notification infrastructure
tasks.description: Generate VAPID keys and store as env vars. Create API route POST /api/push/subscribe to save user's push subscription to DB. Create API route DELETE /api/push/subscribe to unsubscribe. Build a server-side cron job or scheduled function that runs every minute, queries reminders due now, sends Web Push notifications via the web-push npm library, and marks them as notified in the DB.

tasks.title: Extension auth flow
tasks.description: Add login UI to the extension popup: show a 'Sign in' button when not authenticated. Clicking it opens the web app's /login page. After successful magic link auth, the web app passes the session back to the extension (via a /auth/extension callback page that posts the token to the extension using chrome.runtime messaging or a custom protocol). Store session token in chrome.storage.local. Add auth headers to all API calls from the extension.

tasks.title: Extension reminder sync with API
tasks.description: Replace chrome.storage.sync reminder CRUD in the background worker with API calls to the backend. On ADD_REMINDER, POST to API. On DELETE_REMINDER, DELETE to API. On GET_REMINDERS, GET from API. Keep a local cache in chrome.storage.local for offline resilience and fast reads. The extension still runs its 1-minute alarm to check for due reminders locally (belt and suspenders with server-side push).

tasks.title: Extension push subscription registration
tasks.description: When user logs in via the extension, register a Web Push subscription using the service worker's pushManager.subscribe() with the VAPID public key. Send the subscription to POST /api/push/subscribe. Handle push events in the service worker to show notifications (even when extension popup is closed).

tasks.title: Require login before reminder creation
tasks.description: Update the content script's reminder dialog. Before opening the reminder picker, check if the user is authenticated. If not, show a message: 'Sign in to set reminders' with a button that opens the login flow. Block reminder creation for unauthenticated users.

tasks.title: Web dashboard for managing reminders
tasks.description: Build an authenticated dashboard page at /dashboard. Show all user reminders sorted by due date. Display tweet text, author, time remaining/overdue status. Actions: delete reminder, snooze, open tweet. Reuse styling patterns from the extension popup. Protected route - redirect to /login if not authenticated.

tasks.title: Marketing / landing page
tasks.description: Build a public landing page at / that explains the product: set reminders on tweets, get push notifications, never lose reminders. Include: hero section, feature highlights (push notifications, cloud sync, works across devices), how-it-works steps, CTA to install the extension (link to Chrome Web Store or direct download), and a sign-in link for existing users.

tasks.title: Docker setup for Hetzner deployment
tasks.description: Create Dockerfile for the Next.js app (multi-stage build, standalone output). Create docker-compose.yml at repo root with: postgres service (with volume for persistence), web service (Next.js app, depends on postgres), environment variables for DATABASE_URL, RESEND_API_KEY, VAPID keys, BETTER_AUTH_SECRET, etc. Add .env.example documenting all required vars.

tasks.title: Reminder notification cron worker
tasks.description: Implement the server-side process that checks for due reminders and sends push notifications. This could be a Next.js cron route (if using Vercel-style), or more likely a simple setInterval in a custom server entry, or a separate lightweight worker process in Docker. Must handle: querying due reminders, sending web-push to all user subscriptions, marking reminders as server-notified, retry logic for failed pushes, cleaning up expired subscriptions.
